name: yacs

metadata.name: yacs
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation: A simple experiment configuration system for research

variables.name: yacs.tests._ignore
documentation: noqa: F821

modules.name: yacs.config
documentation:
YACS -- Yet Another Configuration System is designed to be a simple
configuration management system for academic and industrial research
projects.

See README.md for usage and examples.

WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.

limitations under the License.

functions.name: yacs.config._merge_a_into_b
documentation:
Merge config dictionary a into config dictionary b, clobbering the
options in b whenever they are also specified in a.

functions.name: yacs.config._check_and_coerce_cfg_value_type
documentation:
Checks that `replacement`, which is intended to replace `original` is of
the right type. The type is correct if it matches exactly or is one of a few
cases in which the type can be easily coerced.

variables.name: yacs.config._VALID_TYPES
documentation: noqa: F821

variables.name: yacs.config.load_cfg
documentation: keep this function in global scope for backward compatibility

classes.name: yacs.config.CfgNode

methods.name: yacs.config.CfgNode.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    init_dict (dict): the possibly-nested dictionary to initailize the CfgNode.
    key_list (list[str]): a list of names which index this CfgNode from the root.
        Currently only used for logging purposes.
    new_allowed (bool): whether adding new key is allowed when merging with
        other configs.

methods.name: yacs.config.CfgNode._create_config_tree_from_dict
documentation:
Create a configuration tree using the given dict.
Any dict-like objects inside dict will be treated as a new CfgNode.

Args:
    dic (dict):
    key_list (list[str]): a list of names which index this CfgNode from the root.
        Currently only used for logging purposes.

methods.name: yacs.config.CfgNode.dump
documentation: Dump to a string.

methods.name: yacs.config.CfgNode.merge_from_file
documentation: Load a yaml config file and merge it this CfgNode.

methods.name: yacs.config.CfgNode.merge_from_other_cfg
documentation: Merge `cfg_other` into this CfgNode.

methods.name: yacs.config.CfgNode.merge_from_list
documentation:
Merge config (keys, values) in a list (e.g., from command line) into
this CfgNode. For example, `cfg_list = ['FOO.BAR', 0.5]`.

methods.name: yacs.config.CfgNode.freeze
documentation: Make this CfgNode and all of its children immutable.

methods.name: yacs.config.CfgNode.defrost
documentation: Make this CfgNode and all of its children mutable.

methods.name: yacs.config.CfgNode.is_frozen
documentation: Return mutability.

methods.name: yacs.config.CfgNode._immutable
documentation:
Set immutability to is_immutable and recursively apply the setting
to all nested CfgNodes.

methods.name: yacs.config.CfgNode.clone
documentation: Recursively copy this CfgNode.

methods.name: yacs.config.CfgNode.register_deprecated_key
documentation:
Register key (e.g. `FOO.BAR`) a deprecated option. When merging deprecated
keys a warning is generated and the key is ignored.

methods.name: yacs.config.CfgNode.register_renamed_key
documentation:
Register a key as having been renamed from `old_name` to `new_name`.
When merging a renamed key, an exception is thrown alerting to user to
the fact that the key has been renamed.

methods.name: yacs.config.CfgNode.key_is_deprecated
documentation: Test if a key is deprecated.

methods.name: yacs.config.CfgNode.key_is_renamed
documentation: Test if a key is renamed.

methods.name: yacs.config.CfgNode.set_new_allowed
documentation:
Set this config (and recursively its subconfigs) to allow merging
new keys from other configs.

methods.name: yacs.config.CfgNode.load_cfg
documentation:
Load a cfg.
Args:
    cfg_file_obj_or_str (str or file):
        Supports loading from:
        - A file object backed by a YAML file
        - A file object backed by a Python source file that exports an attribute
          "cfg" that is either a dict or a CfgNode
        - A string that can be parsed as valid YAML

methods.name: yacs.config.CfgNode._load_cfg_from_file
documentation: Load a config from a YAML file or a Python source file.

methods.name: yacs.config.CfgNode._load_cfg_from_yaml_str
documentation: Load a config from a YAML string encoding.

methods.name: yacs.config.CfgNode._load_cfg_py_source
documentation: Load a config from a Python source file.

methods.name: yacs.config.CfgNode._decode_cfg_value
documentation:
Decodes a raw config value (e.g., from a yaml config files or command
line argument) into a Python object.

If the value is a dict, it will be interpreted as a new CfgNode.
If the value is a str, it will be evaluated as literals.
Otherwise it is returned as-is.

CfgNode represents an internal node in the configuration tree. It's a simple
dict-like container that allows for attribute-based access to keys.
